channel.title: UX AI Daily Digest
channel.description: Daily AI tools, articles, research, prompts, and comics for UX designers.

item.title: Galileo AI
item.description: Galileo AI generates high-fidelity UI designs from natural language prompts, creating editable components and layouts instantly. The tool now supports direct Figma export and has expanded its component library significantly in early 2026. — Dramatically accelerates the ideation-to-mockup phase, letting designers explore more concepts in less time.
item.guid: Galileo AI-20260311

item.title: Figma AI (Make Designs Feature)
item.description: Figma's native AI capabilities now include 'Make Designs' which generates UI layouts, auto-populates realistic content, and suggests design variations based on your existing design system. The February 2026 update added multi-language content generation. — Integrates AI directly into the tool most designers already use daily, reducing context-switching and maintaining design system consistency.
item.guid: Figma AI (Make Designs Feature)-20260311

item.title: Attention Insight 3.0
item.description: This AI-powered predictive eye-tracking tool simulates where users will look on your designs without needing real participants. Version 3.0, released January 2026, added video/animation analysis and improved accuracy to 94% correlation with actual eye-tracking studies. — Provides instant, affordable usability validation that previously required expensive eye-tracking hardware and participant recruitment.
item.guid: Attention Insight 3.0-20260311

item.title: Synthetic Users
item.description: Synthetic Users creates AI-generated research participants that simulate user interviews and usability testing. Their March 2026 update introduced persona customization based on real demographic and psychographic data sets. — Enables rapid early-stage concept testing and hypothesis generation before investing in recruiting real participants.
item.guid: Synthetic Users-20260311

item.title: AI-Assisted Design Systems: How Teams Are Scaling UX Work in 2026
item.description: This comprehensive guide explores how design teams are integrating AI tools directly into their design system workflows. It covers practical examples of using AI for component documentation, accessibility audits, and maintaining consistency across large product ecosystems. — Design systems are foundational to scalable UX work, and understanding how AI can automate the tedious maintenance tasks frees designers for higher-value strategic thinking. [search link]
item.guid: AI-Assisted Design Systems: How Teams Are Scaling UX Work in 2026-20260311

item.title: The UX Designer's AI Toolkit: What's Actually Worth Using
item.description: A critical evaluation of the current landscape of AI tools marketed to UX professionals, separating genuine productivity boosters from overhyped gimmicks. The author shares a practical framework for evaluating whether an AI tool will genuinely improve your workflow or just add complexity. — With hundreds of AI tools flooding the market, designers need clear criteria to invest their learning time wisely and avoid tool fatigue. [search link]
item.guid: The UX Designer's AI Toolkit: What's Actually Worth Using-20260311

item.title: Practical AI for UX Research: From Synthesis to Insights
item.description: This tutorial walks through using AI to accelerate qualitative research analysis, including interview transcript coding, affinity mapping, and theme identification. It includes honest discussion of where AI falls short and still requires human judgment. — Research synthesis is one of the most time-consuming parts of UX work, and learning to responsibly delegate portions to AI can dramatically increase research throughput. [search link]
item.guid: Practical AI for UX Research: From Synthesis to Insights-20260311

item.title: Prompt Engineering for Designers: Writing Instructions That Get Results
item.description: A designer-focused guide to crafting effective prompts for AI tools, covering techniques specific to visual design requests, content generation, and design critique. Includes reusable prompt templates and common pitfalls that lead to generic outputs. — The quality of AI output depends heavily on input quality, making prompt literacy an essential skill for designers who want consistent, useful results from AI collaborators. [search link]
item.guid: Prompt Engineering for Designers: Writing Instructions That Get Results-20260311

item.title: The Impact of Generative AI on UX Design Practice
item.description: This comprehensive study from the Nielsen Norman Group examines how design teams are integrating AI tools into their workflows. Based on interviews with 47 UX professionals, it documents productivity gains averaging 34% for early-stage ideation while highlighting concerns about design homogenization. — Provides evidence-based guidance on where AI tools genuinely accelerate design work versus where human judgment remains essential. [search link]
item.guid: The Impact of Generative AI on UX Design Practice-20260311

item.title: AI-Augmented Design: A Framework for Human-AI Collaboration in UX
item.description: Published in the ACM CHI 2025 proceedings, this paper proposes a structured framework for determining which design tasks benefit from AI assistance. The researchers conducted controlled experiments comparing AI-augmented and traditional design processes across 12 design challenges. — Offers a practical decision framework for knowing when to leverage AI tools and when to rely on traditional methods. [search link]
item.guid: AI-Augmented Design: A Framework for Human-AI Collaboration in UX-20260311

item.title: State of AI in Design 2025: Industry Report
item.description: Figma's annual research report surveying over 4,000 designers worldwide on AI tool adoption, workflow changes, and emerging skill requirements. Key finding: 68% of designers now use AI for user research synthesis, up from 23% in 2023. — Benchmarks your AI adoption against industry trends and identifies skills gaps to address for career growth. [search link]
item.guid: State of AI in Design 2025: Industry Report-20260311

item.title: Cognitive Offloading in AI-Assisted Design: Risks and Mitigations
item.description: MIT Media Lab researchers investigated how designers' critical thinking patterns change when using AI assistants for UX work. The study found measurable decreases in design rationale documentation but suggests intervention strategies to maintain analytical rigor. — Warns against over-reliance on AI and provides concrete practices to maintain your strategic thinking skills. [search link]
item.guid: Cognitive Offloading in AI-Assisted Design: Risks and Mitigations-20260311

item.title: The CRAFT Framework for UX Research Synthesis
item.description: This prompt template uses the Context-Role-Action-Format-Tone structure to help AI synthesize user research findings. It guides you to specify the research type, desired output format (affinity diagrams, themes, or insight statements), and level of detail needed for stakeholder presentations. — Structured prompts reduce the back-and-forth iterations needed to get usable research insights from AI tools. [search link]
item.guid: The CRAFT Framework for UX Research Synthesis-20260311

item.title: Evidence-Based Persona Generator Prompt
item.description: This template walks you through inputting raw user interview data, behavioral patterns, and demographic segments to generate research-backed personas. It includes specific fields for goals, frustrations, technology comfort levels, and quotes pulled directly from your research data. — It prevents the common pitfall of AI-generated personas feeling generic by anchoring outputs in your actual research data. [search link]
item.guid: Evidence-Based Persona Generator Prompt-20260311

item.title: Heuristic Design Critique Prompt Template
item.description: Based on Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics, this prompt template asks AI to evaluate uploaded screenshots or described interfaces against specific criteria. It generates structured feedback with severity ratings, specific problem locations, and actionable recommendations for each violation found. — It provides a consistent, thorough critique framework when you need quick feedback outside of formal design reviews. [search link]
item.guid: Heuristic Design Critique Prompt Template-20260311

item.title: Usability Test Script & Analysis Prompt Pack
item.description: This two-part template first helps generate moderated usability test scripts with task scenarios, probing questions, and success metrics tailored to your product. The second prompt template then analyzes session notes to identify patterns, calculate task completion rates, and prioritize findings by impact. — It cuts usability test prep time significantly while ensuring you capture the right data for meaningful analysis. [search link]
item.guid: Usability Test Script & Analysis Prompt Pack-20260311

item.title: 2026 UX Industry Report: AI Collaboration Skills Now Required in 73% of Senior Roles
item.description: Nielsen Norman Group's annual report reveals that AI collaboration has become a core competency in UX hiring. Companies are specifically seeking designers who can craft effective prompts, validate AI outputs, and design human-AI interaction patterns. — Understanding where hiring managers are focusing helps you prioritize which AI skills to develop and highlight in your portfolio. [search link]
item.guid: 2026 UX Industry Report: AI Collaboration Skills Now Required in 73% of Senior Roles-20260311

item.title: Figma's AI Features Drive New 'AI Design Ops' Specialty Role
item.description: Since Figma's expanded AI toolkit launch in late 2025, a new hybrid role has emerged combining design systems management with AI workflow optimization. LinkedIn reports a 340% increase in job postings mentioning 'AI Design Ops' since January. — Emerging role categories represent opportunities for early career positioning before the talent pool becomes saturated.
item.guid: Figma's AI Features Drive New 'AI Design Ops' Specialty Role-20260311

item.title: Tech Layoffs Slow as Companies Rebuild UX Teams with AI-Augmented Structure
item.description: After two years of cuts, Q1 2026 shows a 28% increase in UX hiring, but with restructured teams. Companies are hiring fewer designers overall but paying 15-20% premiums for those with demonstrated AI tool proficiency. — The job market recovery favors designers who've invested in AI upskilling, making continuous learning a career resilience strategy. [search link]
item.guid: Tech Layoffs Slow as Companies Rebuild UX Teams with AI-Augmented Structure-20260311

item.title: UXPA Survey: 61% of Designers Report AI Changed Their Daily Workflow
item.description: The User Experience Professionals Association's member survey shows most designers now use AI for research synthesis, copy generation, and accessibility audits. However, 44% express concern about skill atrophy in areas AI now handles. — Knowing how peers are adapting helps you benchmark your own AI integration and identify skills worth maintaining versus delegating. [search link]
item.guid: UXPA Survey: 61% of Designers Report AI Changed Their Daily Workflow-20260311

item.title: XKCD #2054: Data Pipeline
item.description: "Is the pipeline literally running from your laptop?" "Don't be silly, my laptop disconnects far too often to host a service we rely on. It's running on my phone." — https://imgs.xkcd.com/comics/data_pipeline.png
item.guid: xkcd-2054
